{{Shrine |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}}上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・{{ill|Bun'an era|ja|文安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'an era|qq=}} |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. It is venerated by local residents as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}} of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|qq=}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|qq=}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the {{ill|Bun'an era|ja|文安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'an era|qq=}} when {{ill|Emperor Gokaen|ja|後花園天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Gokaen|qq=}} reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明 (日本)|lt=Bunmei era|lt_ja=文明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei era|qq=}}, it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|qq=}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the {{ill|Arisugawa-no-miya|ja|有栖川宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa-no-miya|qq=}}, with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|qq=}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1906|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|qq=}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|qq=}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}—were consolidated between the following year and {{ill|1912|ja|1912nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1912|qq=}} ({{ill|Taisho|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho|qq=}} 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}】. === Chronological Timeline === * {{ill|1811|ja|1811年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1811|qq=}} ({{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)|lt=Bunka|lt_ja=文化|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka era|qq=}} 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|qq=}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|qq=}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}}.The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * {{ill|1873|ja|1873rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|qq=}}. * {{ill|1995|ja|1995年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}}. * {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|qq=}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|lt=Izanagi|lt_ja=伊耶那岐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}}・{{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|lt=Izanami|lt_ja=伊耶那美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotonohirahime|ja|大物主|lt=Kotonohirahime|lt_ja=琴刀比羅神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonohirahime|qq=}}. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|qq=}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|qq=}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|qq=}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|qq=}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant {{ill|Black Pine|ja|クロマツ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Pine|qq=}} trees said to have been growing since around {{ill|1400|ja|1400年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1400|qq=}}, have been designated by {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}} as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|qq=}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}} and was rebuilt in March {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|qq=}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|qq=}} donated in November {{ill|1867|ja|1867年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1867|qq=}} ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 3) by {{ill|Prince Arisugawa|ja|有栖川宮熾仁親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arisugawa|qq=}}, but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|qq=}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|qq=}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|qq=}}, {{ill|Orochi-Hiko no Kami|ja|岐の神|lt=Orochi-Hiko no Kami|lt_ja=八岐比古神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi-Hiko no Kami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Orochi-Hime no Kami|ja|岐の神|lt=Orochi-Hime no Kami|lt_ja=八岐姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi-Hime no Kami|qq=}}. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|qq=}}) from the battleship {{ill|Battleship Aki|ja|安芸 (戦艦)|lt=Battleship Aki|lt_ja=戦艦安芸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battleship Aki|qq=}}, which was decommissioned in {{ill|1923|ja|1923rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the {{ill|Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=}} from Naruo Village.In {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|qq=}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|qq=}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at {{ill|Hanshin Electric Railway|ja|阪神電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin Electric Railway|qq=}}’s {{ill|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station|ja|鳴尾・武庫川女子大前駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station|qq=}} and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s {{ill|Koshien Station|ja|甲子園駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshien Station|qq=}} and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Shrine |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}}上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・{{ill|Bun'an era|ja|文安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'an era|qq=}} |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. It is venerated by local residents as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}} of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|qq=}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|qq=}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the {{ill|Bun'an era|ja|文安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'an era|qq=}} when {{ill|Emperor Gokaen|ja|後花園天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Gokaen|qq=}} reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明 (日本)|lt=Bunmei era|lt_ja=文明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei era|qq=}}, it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|qq=}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the {{ill|Arisugawa-no-miya|ja|有栖川宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa-no-miya|qq=}}, with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|qq=}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1906|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|qq=}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|qq=}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}—were consolidated between the following year and {{ill|1912|ja|1912nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1912|qq=}} ({{ill|Taisho|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho|qq=}} 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}】. === Chronological Timeline === * {{ill|1811|ja|1811年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1811|qq=}} ({{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)|lt=Bunka|lt_ja=文化|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka era|qq=}} 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|qq=}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|qq=}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}}.The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * {{ill|1873|ja|1873年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|qq=}}. * {{ill|1995|ja|1995年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}}. * {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|qq=}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|lt=Izanagi|lt_ja=伊耶那岐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}}・{{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|lt=Izanami|lt_ja=伊耶那美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotonohirahime|ja|大物主|lt=Kotonohirahime|lt_ja=琴刀比羅神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonohirahime|qq=}}. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|qq=}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|qq=}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|qq=}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|qq=}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant {{ill|Black Pine|ja|クロマツ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Pine|qq=}} trees said to have been growing since around {{ill|1400|ja|1400年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1400|qq=}}, have been designated by {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}} as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|qq=}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}} and was rebuilt in March {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|qq=}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|qq=}} donated in November {{ill|1867|ja|1867年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1867|qq=}} ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 3) by {{ill|Prince Arisugawa|ja|有栖川宮熾仁親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arisugawa|qq=}}, but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|qq=}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|qq=}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|qq=}}, {{ill|Orochi-Hiko no Kami|ja|岐の神|lt=Orochi-Hiko no Kami|lt_ja=八岐比古神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi-Hiko no Kami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Orochi-Hime no Kami|ja|岐の神|lt=Orochi-Hime no Kami|lt_ja=八岐姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi-Hime no Kami|qq=}}. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|qq=}}) from the battleship {{ill|Battleship Aki|ja|安芸 (戦艦)|lt=Battleship Aki|lt_ja=戦艦安芸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battleship Aki|qq=}}, which was decommissioned in {{ill|1923|ja|1923年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the {{ill|Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=}} from Naruo Village.In {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|qq=}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|qq=}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at {{ill|Hanshin Electric Railway|ja|阪神電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin Electric Railway|qq=}}’s {{ill|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station|ja|鳴尾・武庫川女子大前駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station|qq=}} and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s {{ill|Koshien Station|ja|甲子園駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshien Station|qq=}} and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Shrine |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}}上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・{{ill|Bun'an era|ja|文安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'an era|qq=}} |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. It is venerated by local residents as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}} of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|qq=}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|qq=}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the {{ill|Bun'an era|ja|文安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'an era|qq=}} when {{ill|Emperor Gokaen|ja|後花園天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Gokaen|qq=}} reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明 (日本)|lt=Bunmei era|lt_ja=文明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei era|qq=}}, it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|qq=}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the {{ill|Arisugawa-no-miya|ja|有栖川宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa-no-miya|qq=}}, with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|qq=}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1906|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|qq=}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|qq=}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}—were consolidated between the following year and {{ill|1912|ja|1912年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1912|qq=}} ({{ill|Taisho|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho|qq=}} 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}】. === Chronological Timeline === * {{ill|1811|ja|1811年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1811|qq=}} ({{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)|lt=Bunka|lt_ja=文化|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka era|qq=}} 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|qq=}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|qq=}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}}.The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * {{ill|1873|ja|1873年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|qq=}}. * {{ill|1995|ja|1995年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}}. * {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|qq=}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|lt=Izanagi|lt_ja=伊耶那岐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}}・{{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|lt=Izanami|lt_ja=伊耶那美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotonohirahime|ja|大物主|lt=Kotonohirahime|lt_ja=琴刀比羅神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonohirahime|qq=}}. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|qq=}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|qq=}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|qq=}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|qq=}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant {{ill|Black Pine|ja|クロマツ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Pine|qq=}} trees said to have been growing since around {{ill|1400|ja|1400年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1400|qq=}}, have been designated by {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}} as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|qq=}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}} and was rebuilt in March {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|qq=}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|qq=}} donated in November {{ill|1867|ja|1867年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1867|qq=}} ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 3) by {{ill|Prince Arisugawa|ja|有栖川宮熾仁親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arisugawa|qq=}}, but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|qq=}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|qq=}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|qq=}}, {{ill|Orochi-Hiko no Kami|ja|岐の神|lt=Orochi-Hiko no Kami|lt_ja=八岐比古神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi-Hiko no Kami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Orochi-Hime no Kami|ja|岐の神|lt=Orochi-Hime no Kami|lt_ja=八岐姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi-Hime no Kami|qq=}}. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|qq=}}) from the battleship {{ill|Battleship Aki|ja|安芸 (戦艦)|lt=Battleship Aki|lt_ja=戦艦安芸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battleship Aki|qq=}}, which was decommissioned in {{ill|1923|ja|1923年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the {{ill|Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=}} from Naruo Village.In {{ill|1941|ja|1941st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|qq=}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|qq=}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at {{ill|Hanshin Electric Railway|ja|阪神電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin Electric Railway|qq=}}’s {{ill|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station|ja|鳴尾・武庫川女子大前駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station|qq=}} and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s {{ill|Koshien Station|ja|甲子園駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshien Station|qq=}} and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Shrine |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}}上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・{{ill|Bun'an era|ja|文安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'an era|qq=}} |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}. It is venerated by local residents as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guardian Deity|qq=}} of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|qq=}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|qq=}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the {{ill|Bun'an era|ja|文安|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bun'an era|qq=}} when {{ill|Emperor Gokaen|ja|後花園天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Gokaen|qq=}} reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the {{ill|Bunmei era|ja|文明 (日本)|lt=Bunmei era|lt_ja=文明|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunmei era|qq=}}, it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|qq=}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the {{ill|Arisugawa-no-miya|ja|有栖川宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arisugawa-no-miya|qq=}}, with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|qq=}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in {{ill|1906|ja|1906年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1906|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|qq=}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|qq=}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}—were consolidated between the following year and {{ill|1912|ja|1912年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1912|qq=}} ({{ill|Taisho|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisho|qq=}} 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}】. === Chronological Timeline === * {{ill|1811|ja|1811年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1811|qq=}} ({{ill|Bunka era|ja|文化 (元号)|lt=Bunka|lt_ja=文化|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunka era|qq=}} 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|qq=}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|qq=}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}}.The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * {{ill|1873|ja|1873年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1873|qq=}} ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|qq=}}. * {{ill|1995|ja|1995年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1995|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}}. * {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|qq=}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ({{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|lt=Izanagi|lt_ja=伊耶那岐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=}}・{{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|lt=Izanami|lt_ja=伊耶那美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}}), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kotonohirahime|ja|大物主|lt=Kotonohirahime|lt_ja=琴刀比羅神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonohirahime|qq=}}. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|qq=}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|qq=}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|qq=}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|qq=}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　{{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant {{ill|Black Pine|ja|クロマツ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Black Pine|qq=}} trees said to have been growing since around {{ill|1400|ja|1400年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1400|qq=}}, have been designated by {{ill|Nishinomiya City|ja|西宮市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya City|qq=}} as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|qq=}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}} and was rebuilt in March {{ill|2000|ja|2000年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2000|qq=}} ({{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|qq=}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|qq=}} donated in November {{ill|1867|ja|1867年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1867|qq=}} ({{ill|Keiō|ja|慶応|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keiō|qq=}} 3) by {{ill|Prince Arisugawa|ja|有栖川宮熾仁親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Arisugawa|qq=}}, but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|qq=}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}}. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|qq=}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}} (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|qq=}}, {{ill|Orochi-Hiko no Kami|ja|岐の神|lt=Orochi-Hiko no Kami|lt_ja=八岐比古神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi-Hiko no Kami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Orochi-Hime no Kami|ja|岐の神|lt=Orochi-Hime no Kami|lt_ja=八岐姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Orochi-Hime no Kami|qq=}}. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second {{ill|Torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torii|qq=}}. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|qq=}}) from the battleship {{ill|Battleship Aki|ja|安芸 (戦艦)|lt=Battleship Aki|lt_ja=戦艦安芸|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Battleship Aki|qq=}}, which was decommissioned in {{ill|1923|ja|1923年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1923|qq=}} (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the {{ill|Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=}} from Naruo Village.In {{ill|1941|ja|1941年|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1941|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|qq=}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|qq=}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at {{ill|Hanshin Electric Railway|ja|阪神電気鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin Electric Railway|qq=}}’s {{ill|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station|ja|鳴尾・武庫川女子大前駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station|qq=}} and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s {{ill|Koshien Station|ja|甲子園駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshien Station|qq=}} and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |last= |first= |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |translator= |editor= Naruo Village Chronicles Editorial Committee|date=2005-03 |title=Naruo Village Chronicles 1885-1951 |publisher=Nishinomiya City Naruo Ward Asset Management Committee |page= |id= |isbn= |quote= |ref=鳴尾村誌}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okata Shrine|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|lt=Okata Shrine|lt_ja=岡太神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6302025.html Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – Hyōgo Prefecture {{ill|Shrine Agency|ja|神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Agency|qq=}} * [https://yaokami.jp/1280901/ Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – [https://yaokami.jp/ Eight Million Gods] * Naruo Hachiman Shrine Volunteer Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|鳴尾八幡神社|version=103027411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| revision.text:
{{Shrine |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]]上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・[[:en:Bun'an|Bun'an era]] |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It is venerated by local residents as the [[:en:Chinjugami|Guardian Deity]] of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|qq=}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|qq=}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the [[:en:Bun'an|Bun'an era]] when [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Gokaen]] reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei era]], it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|qq=}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]], with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|qq=}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|qq=}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|qq=}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}—were consolidated between the following year and [[:en:1912|1912]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|qq=}}、[[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]】. === Chronological Timeline === * [[:en:1811|1811]] ([[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|qq=}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|qq=}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]].The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * [[:en:1873|1873]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|qq=}}. * [[:en:1995|1995]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}}. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|qq=}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|qq=}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|qq=}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|qq=}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ([[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]]・[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Ōmononushi|Kotonohirahime]]. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|qq=}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|qq=}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|qq=}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|qq=}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　[[:en:Torii|Torii]]]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Black Pine]] trees said to have been growing since around [[:en:1400|1400]], have been designated by [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost [[:en:Torii|Torii]] to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|qq=}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|qq=}} and was rebuilt in March [[:en:2000|2000]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|qq=}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|qq=}} donated in November [[:en:1867|1867]] ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3) by [[:en:Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Prince Arisugawa]], but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|qq=}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|qq=}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|qq=}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third [[:en:Torii|Torii]] (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|qq=}}, [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hiko no Kami]], and [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hime no Kami]]. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second [[:en:Torii|Torii]]. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|qq=}}) from the battleship [[:en:Japanese battleship Aki|Battleship Aki]], which was decommissioned in [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|qq=}} from Naruo Village.In [[:en:1941|1941]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|qq=}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|qq=}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at [[:en:Hanshin Electric Railway|Hanshin Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Naruo - Mukogawajoshidai-Mae Station|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station]] and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s [[:en:Kōshien Station|Koshien Station]] and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |last= |first= |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |translator= |editor= Naruo Village Chronicles Editorial Committee|date=2005-03 |title=Naruo Village Chronicles 1885-1951 |publisher=Nishinomiya City Naruo Ward Asset Management Committee |page= |id= |isbn= |quote= |ref=鳴尾村誌}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okata Shrine|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|lt=Okata Shrine|lt_ja=岡太神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6302025.html Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – Hyōgo Prefecture {{ill|Shrine Agency|ja|神社庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Agency|qq=}} * [https://yaokami.jp/1280901/ Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – [https://yaokami.jp/ Eight Million Gods] * Naruo Hachiman Shrine Volunteer Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|鳴尾八幡神社|version=103027411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman Shrines]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya City]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] }} [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]]
