The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1114013}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575740}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|1=Isotakeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10881556}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third [[:en:Torii|Torii]] (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ|1=Omoikane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q586088}}, [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hiko no Kami]], and [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hime no Kami]]. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second [[:en:Torii|Torii]]. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銃砲身|1=gun barrel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q487192}}) from the battleship [[:en:Japanese battleship Aki|Battleship Aki]], which was decommissioned in [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}} from Naruo Village.In [[:en:1941|1941]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|1=Metal Collection Order|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646956}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|1=shinme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591047}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at [[:en:Hanshin Electric Railway|Hanshin Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Naruo - Mukogawajoshidai-Mae Station|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station]] and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s [[:en:Kōshien Station|Koshien Station]] and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |last= |first= |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |translator= |editor= Naruo Village Chronicles Editorial Committee|date=2005-03 |title=Naruo Village Chronicles 1885-1951 |publisher=Nishinomiya City Naruo Ward Asset Management Committee |page= |id= |isbn= |quote= |ref=鳴尾村誌}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okata Shrine|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|lt=Okata Shrine|lt_ja=岡太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata Shrine|1=Okata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472009}} == External Links == * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6302025.html Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – Hyōgo Prefecture {{ill|Shrine Agency|ja|神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Agency|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://yaokami.jp/1280901/ Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – [https://yaokami.jp/ Eight Million Gods] * Naruo Hachiman Shrine Volunteer Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|鳴尾八幡神社|version=103027411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119811}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119811]] Wikidata name: Naruo hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]]上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・[[:en:Bun'an|Bun'an era]] |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |image = File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Shaden.jpg |prefecture = {{ill|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.722222|N|135.368972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It is venerated by local residents as the [[:en:Chinjugami|Guardian Deity]] of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|1=Shrine Name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588355}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the [[:en:Bun'an|Bun'an era]] when [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Gokaen]] reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei era]], it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|1=Naruo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675332}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]], with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|1=Shrine Merger Movement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590700}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|1=Muko district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545988}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—were consolidated between the following year and [[:en:1912|1912]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、[[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]】. === Chronological Timeline === * [[:en:1811|1811]] ([[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瓦葺き|1=tiled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855730}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]].The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * [[:en:1873|1873]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=shrine rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444029}}. * [[:en:1995|1995]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}}. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|1=saijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591100}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現|1=Kasuga Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11088804}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ([[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]]・[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, [[:en:Ōmononushi|Kotonohirahime]]. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|Narao Hachiman Shrine#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　[[:en:Torii|Torii]]]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Black Pine]] trees said to have been growing since around [[:en:1400|1400]], have been designated by [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost [[:en:Torii|Torii]] to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|1=LaLaport Koshien|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11236474}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} and was rebuilt in March [[:en:2000|2000]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|1=imperial plaque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071212}} donated in November [[:en:1867|1867]] ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3) by [[:en:Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Prince Arisugawa]], but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1114013}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575740}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|1=Isotakeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10881556}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third [[:en:Torii|Torii]] (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ|1=Omoikane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q586088}}, [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hiko no Kami]], and [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hime no Kami]]. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second [[:en:Torii|Torii]]. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銃砲身|1=gun barrel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q487192}}) from the battleship [[:en:Japanese battleship Aki|Battleship Aki]], which was decommissioned in [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}} from Naruo Village.In [[:en:1941|1941]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|1=Metal Collection Order|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646956}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|1=shinme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591047}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at [[:en:Hanshin Electric Railway|Hanshin Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Naruo - Mukogawajoshidai-Mae Station|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station]] and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s [[:en:Kōshien Station|Koshien Station]] and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |last= |first= |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |translator= |editor= Naruo Village Chronicles Editorial Committee|date=2005-03 |title=Naruo Village Chronicles 1885-1951 |publisher=Nishinomiya City Naruo Ward Asset Management Committee |page= |id= |isbn= |quote= |ref=鳴尾村誌}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okata Shrine|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|lt=Okata Shrine|lt_ja=岡太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata Shrine|1=Okata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472009}} == External Links == * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6302025.html Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – Hyōgo Prefecture {{ill|Shrine Agency|ja|神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Agency|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://yaokami.jp/1280901/ Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – [https://yaokami.jp/ Eight Million Gods] * Naruo Hachiman Shrine Volunteer Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|鳴尾八幡神社|version=103027411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119811}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119811]] Wikidata name: Naruo hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]]上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・[[:en:Bun'an|Bun'an era]] |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |image = File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Shaden.jpg |prefecture = {{ill|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.722222|N|135.368972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It is venerated by local residents as the [[:en:Chinjugami|Guardian Deity]] of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|1=Shrine Name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588355}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the [[:en:Bun'an|Bun'an era]] when [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Gokaen]] reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei era]], it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|1=Naruo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675332}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]], with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|1=Shrine Merger Movement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590700}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|1=Muko district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545988}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—were consolidated between the following year and [[:en:1912|1912]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、[[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]】. === Chronological Timeline === * [[:en:1811|1811]] ([[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瓦葺き|1=tiled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855730}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]].The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * [[:en:1873|1873]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=shrine rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444029}}. * [[:en:1995|1995]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}}. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|1=saijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591100}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現|1=Kasuga Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11088804}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ([[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]]・[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, [[:en:Ōmononushi|Kotonohirahime]]. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|Narao Hachiman Shrine#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　[[:en:Torii|Torii]]]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Black Pine]] trees said to have been growing since around [[:en:1400|1400]], have been designated by [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost [[:en:Torii|Torii]] to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|1=LaLaport Koshien|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11236474}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} and was rebuilt in March [[:en:2000|2000]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|1=imperial plaque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071212}} donated in November [[:en:1867|1867]] ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3) by [[:en:Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Prince Arisugawa]], but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1114013}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575740}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|1=Isotakeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10881556}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third [[:en:Torii|Torii]] (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ|1=Omoikane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q586088}}, [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hiko no Kami]], and [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hime no Kami]]. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second [[:en:Torii|Torii]]. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銃砲身|1=gun barrel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q487192}}) from the battleship [[:en:Japanese battleship Aki|Battleship Aki]], which was decommissioned in [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}} from Naruo Village.In [[:en:1941|1941]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|1=Metal Collection Order|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646956}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|1=shinme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591047}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at [[:en:Hanshin Electric Railway|Hanshin Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Naruo - Mukogawajoshidai-Mae Station|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station]] and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s [[:en:Kōshien Station|Koshien Station]] and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |last= |first= |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |translator= |editor= Naruo Village Chronicles Editorial Committee|date=2005-03 |title=Naruo Village Chronicles 1885-1951 |publisher=Nishinomiya City Naruo Ward Asset Management Committee |page= |id= |isbn= |quote= |ref=鳴尾村誌}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okata Shrine|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|lt=Okata Shrine|lt_ja=岡太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata Shrine|1=Okata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472009}} == External Links == * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6302025.html Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – Hyōgo Prefecture {{ill|Shrine Agency|ja|神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Agency|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://yaokami.jp/1280901/ Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – [https://yaokami.jp/ Eight Million Gods] * Naruo Hachiman Shrine Volunteer Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|鳴尾八幡神社|version=103027411|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119811}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119811]] Wikidata name: Naruo hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]]上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・[[:en:Bun'an|Bun'an era]] |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |image = File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Shaden.jpg |prefecture = {{ill|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.722222|N|135.368972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It is venerated by local residents as the [[:en:Chinjugami|Guardian Deity]] of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|1=Shrine Name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588355}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the [[:en:Bun'an|Bun'an era]] when [[:en:Emperor Go-Hanazono|Emperor Gokaen]] reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the [[:en:Bunmei|Bunmei era]], it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|1=Naruo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675332}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the [[:en:Arisugawa-no-miya|Arisugawa-no-miya]], with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|1=Shrine Merger Movement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590700}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|1=Muko district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545988}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—were consolidated between the following year and [[:en:1912|1912]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taisho]] 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、[[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]]】. === Chronological Timeline === * [[:en:1811|1811]] ([[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瓦葺き|1=tiled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855730}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]].The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * [[:en:1873|1873]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=shrine rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444029}}. * [[:en:1995|1995]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}}. * [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|1=saijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591100}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現|1=Kasuga Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11088804}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ([[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]]・[[:en:Izanami|Izanami]]), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, [[:en:Ōmononushi|Kotonohirahime]]. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|Narao Hachiman Shrine#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　[[:en:Torii|Torii]]]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant [[:en:Pinus thunbergii|Black Pine]] trees said to have been growing since around [[:en:1400|1400]], have been designated by [[:en:Nishinomiya|Nishinomiya City]] as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost [[:en:Torii|Torii]] to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|1=LaLaport Koshien|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11236474}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} and was rebuilt in March [[:en:2000|2000]] ([[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|1=imperial plaque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071212}} donated in November [[:en:1867|1867]] ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3) by [[:en:Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|Prince Arisugawa]], but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]]. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1114013}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575740}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|1=Isotakeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10881556}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third [[:en:Torii|Torii]] (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ|1=Omoikane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q586088}}, [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hiko no Kami]], and [[:en:Kunado-no-Kami|Orochi-Hime no Kami]]. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second [[:en:Torii|Torii]]. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銃砲身|1=gun barrel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q487192}}) from the battleship [[:en:Japanese battleship Aki|Battleship Aki]], which was decommissioned in [[:en:1923|1923]] (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}} from Naruo Village.In [[:en:1941|1941]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|1=Metal Collection Order|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646956}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|1=shinme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591047}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at [[:en:Hanshin Electric Railway|Hanshin Electric Railway]]’s [[:en:Naruo - Mukogawajoshidai-Mae Station|Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station]] and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s [[:en:Kōshien Station|Koshien Station]] and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |last= |first= |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |translator= |editor= Naruo Village Chronicles Editorial Committee|date=2005-03 |title=Naruo Village Chronicles 1885-1951 |publisher=Nishinomiya City Naruo Ward Asset Management Committee |page= |id= |isbn= |quote= |ref=鳴尾村誌}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okata Shrine|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|lt=Okata Shrine|lt_ja=岡太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata Shrine|1=Okata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472009}} == External Links == * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6302025.html Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – Hyōgo Prefecture {{ill|Shrine Agency|ja|神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Agency|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://yaokami.jp/1280901/ Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – [https://yaokami.jp/ Eight Million Gods] * Naruo Hachiman Shrine Volunteer Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|鳴尾八幡神社|version=103027411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119811}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119811]] Wikidata name: Naruo hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}}上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・{{ill|Bun'an|en|Bun'an|WD=Q1193586|lt=Bun'an era}} |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |image = File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Shaden.jpg |prefecture = {{ill|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.722222|N|135.368972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is venerated by local residents as the {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|WD=Q97479976|lt=Guardian Deity}} of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|1=Shrine Name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588355}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the {{ill|Bun'an|en|Bun'an|WD=Q1193586|lt=Bun'an era}} when {{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Gokaen}} reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮). During the {{ill|Bunmei|en|Bunmei|WD=Q1193544|lt=Bunmei era}}, it became the general guardian shrine of seven villages including {{ill|Naruo Village|ja|鳴尾村 (兵庫県)|lt=Naruo Village|lt_ja=鳴尾村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village|1=Naruo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11675332}}[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/kimmy_v-kobe/e/b54cc58dd416927eb59f8e688e693903 西宮・鳴尾八幡神社。] - 神戸の空の下で。～街角の歴史発見～ and was called '''Hongo no Miya'''. In addition, it has been revered for generations by the {{ill|Arisugawa-no-miya|en|Arisugawa-no-miya|WD=Q2861427|lt=Arisugawa-no-miya}}, with ritual offerings ({{ill|Offerings|ja|幣帛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Offerings|1=heihaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481885}}) and songs frequently donated. Furthermore, in {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 39), the {{ill|Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|ja|神社合祀#明治末期の神社合祀|lt=Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|lt_ja=神社合祀令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine Consolidation Order|1=Shrine Merger Movement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590700}} was promulgated, and as the village shrine and general guardian of Naruo Village in {{ill|Muko District|ja|武庫郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muko District|1=Muko district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11545988}}, several shrines within Naruo Village—including {{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—were consolidated between the following year and {{ill|1912|en|1912|WD=Q2077|lt=1912}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taisho}} 1)【{{ill|Susanoo Shrine|ja|素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo Shrine|qq=}}、{{ill|Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja|田ノ前八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanomae Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja|西浜素盞嗚神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Susanoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja|外葭島琴刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotomakishima Kotonohirahime Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Sunahama Shrine|ja|砂浜神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunahama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}、{{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}}】. === Chronological Timeline === * {{ill|1811|en|1811|WD=Q6928|lt=1811}} ({{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 8): The shrine buildings were rebuilt. The worship hall was tiled ({{ill|Tiled roof|ja|瓦葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tiled roof|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瓦葺き|1=tiled roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10855730}}) and the main hall was roofed with cypress bark ({{ill|Cypress bark roof|ja|檜皮葺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cypress bark roof|1=cypress bark roof|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q90373477}}) in a three-bay layout; it was constructed in the rare style of {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}}.The front of the main hall features three intercolumn spaces. * {{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 6) August: It was classified as a village shrine under the {{ill|Modern Shrine Ranking System|ja|社格#近代社格制度|lt=Modern Shrine Ranking System|lt_ja=近代社格制度|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern Shrine Ranking System|1=shrine rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10444029}}. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 7): The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}}. * {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} (Heisei 12) March: The shrine buildings were rebuilt. * September 2018 (Heisei 30): The main hall roof was damaged by a typhoon. * December 26, 2021 (Reiwa 3): Mr. Yoshii Yoshiaki, the new head priest of {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|WD=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine}}, assumed office. * June 16, 2022 (Reiwa 4): Repair work on the worship hall roof was completed. * June 25, 2022 (Reiwa 4): A ceremony was held to transfer from the temporary main hall to the main hall. == Enshrined Deities == * At the time of its founding, as the shrine was near the sea and the Naruo sea was treacherous, many of the {{ill|Enshrined Deities|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enshrined Deities|1=saijin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591100}} are gods associated with the sea. * In the shrine buildings – Main Deities: {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}; Auxiliary Deities: {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1067028}}, {{ill|Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja|春日大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 春日権現|1=Kasuga Gongen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11088804}}, Iwaya Daimyōjin ({{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}}・{{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}}), {{ill|Himuro Daimyōjin|ja|氷室大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Daimyōjin|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|lt=Wakamiya Daimyōjin|lt_ja=若宮大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Daimyōjin|qq=}}, {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Kotonohirahime}}. * Onsite Shrines – ''See the section on {{ill|Shrine Grounds|ja|Narao Hachiman Shrine#境内|lt=Shrine Grounds|lt_ja=境内|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Grounds|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} below for details.'' == Rituals == * January 1 – {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} * March 25 – Tenmangu Annual Festival * July 9 – Summer Festival * July 15 – {{ill|Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja|猿田彦社祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Shrine Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * August 15 – Memorial Festival * August 24 – {{ill|Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja|子安社例祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koyasu Shrine Annual Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * October 20 – Autumn Festival * Daily 9:30 – Daily Offering Ceremony (にっくさい) == Shrine Grounds == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Torii.jpg|thumb|200px|南側　{{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}}]] [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Sando.jpg|thumb|200px|境内　参道]] The grounds, covering approximately 2,000 tsubo and flourishing with ancient giant {{ill|Pinus thunbergii|en|Pinus thunbergii|WD=Q854884|lt=Black Pine}} trees said to have been growing since around {{ill|1400|en|1400|WD=Q6108|lt=1400}}, have been designated by {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}} as a '''Scenic Tree Conservation District''' (景観樹林保護地区).{{pdf|[http://www.nishi.or.jp/media/2010/jyurin.pdf 景観樹林保護地区指定一覧表] - 西宮市ホームページ}} A nearly 170‑meter-long approach extends straight from the southernmost {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} to in front of the shrine buildings;It is said that in the past the approach extended further to the southern coast (present-day near {{ill|LaLaport Koshien|ja|ららぽーと甲子園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/LaLaport Koshien|1=LaLaport Koshien|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11236474}}). Along this path, subsidiary shrines and a memorial tower are located. ; Shrine Buildings : Constructed with a wooden structure and copper-plate roofing. The main hall was completely destroyed by the {{ill|Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|ja|阪神・淡路大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hanshin-Awaji Earthquake|1=Damage by Great Hanshin earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392882}} and was rebuilt in March {{ill|2000|en|2000|WD=Q1985|lt=2000}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 12). The inscription tablet ({{ill|Inscription Tablet|ja|扁額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inscription Tablet|1=biane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5358873}}) that once adorned the worship hall was an {{ill|Imperial Decree|ja|勅額|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Decree|1=imperial plaque|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11071212}} donated in November {{ill|1867|en|1867|WD=Q7715|lt=1867}} ({{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 3) by {{ill|Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|en|Prince Arisugawa Taruhito|WD=Q668338|lt=Prince Arisugawa}}, but after being damaged in the earthquake, a replica now hangs in its place. ; Inari Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach in front of the shrine buildings. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kura Inadama-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}. ; Tenmangu Shrine : Situated on the west side of the approach, immediately north of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}}. ; Sarutahiko Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, immediately south of the Inari Shrine. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja|住吉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1114013}}, {{ill|Funadama Ōkami|ja|船玉大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funadama Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Matsuo Ōkami|ja|松尾大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}}, {{ill|Izumo Ōkami|ja|出雲大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tashima Ōkami|ja|淡島神|lt=Tashima Ōkami|lt_ja=淡島大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Ōkami|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575740}}, {{ill|Hiromine Ōkami|ja|五十猛神|lt=Hiromine Ōkami|lt_ja=廣峯大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiromine Ōkami|1=Isotakeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10881556}}, {{ill|Taga Ōkami|ja|多賀大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taga Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tato Ōkami|ja|多度大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tato Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ichimokuren Ōkami|ja|天目一箇神#一目連|lt=Ichimokuren Ōkami|lt_ja=一目連大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichimokuren Ōkami|1=Ame no Mahitotsu no kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10016750}}.[http://bcbweb.bai.ne.jp/naruohachiman/matusyaprofile023.html 猿田彦社の御祭神] - 公式サイト ; Koyasu Shrine : Located on the west side of the approach, south of the third {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}} (石橋). The enshrined deities are {{ill|Konohanasakuya-hime|ja|木花咲耶姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konohanasakuya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to コノハナノサクヤビメ}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|磐長姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ}}, {{ill|Yatsukamui|ja|八意思兼神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yatsukamui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ|1=Omoikane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q586088}}, {{ill|Kunado-no-Kami|en|Kunado-no-Kami|WD=Q2963653|lt=Orochi-Hiko no Kami}}, and {{ill|Kunado-no-Kami|en|Kunado-no-Kami|WD=Q2963653|lt=Orochi-Hime no Kami}}. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine : Annual festival is held on September 10. ; Naruo Motohayabusa Shrine (Additional Tradition) : In Naruo, during the portable deity procession to Nishinomiya where the deity Hiruko is carried, there is a tradition of fishermen who offered their services. These people were known as “Taiyū”, and their descendants inherited the title. It is said there were seven Taiyū households. Due to wartime destruction burning the records of temples and houses, it is now unknown which families originally held the title; however, the collective leader of the Taiyū participants was called “Sōtaiyū”. (Omitted) Prior to the war, Naruo’s Taiyū also participated in Nishinomiya Shrine’s portable deity procession on May 14 (old calendar). The “Sōtaiyū” mentioned here still participate in festivals and offer watermelons, a local specialty of Naruo. ; Memorial Tower : Located on the west side of the approach, north of the second {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii}}. It was constructed using a cannon barrel ({{ill|Cannon Barrel|ja|砲身|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cannon Barrel|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銃砲身|1=gun barrel|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q487192}}) from the battleship {{ill|Japanese battleship Aki|en|Japanese battleship Aki|WD=Q2491891|lt=Battleship Aki}}, which was decommissioned in {{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923}} (Taisho 12). Its old name was '''Loyalty Monument'''. It enshrines those who died in the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, and the {{ill|Greater East Asia War|ja|大東亜戦争|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greater East Asia War|1=Greater East Asia War|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3846875}} from Naruo Village.In {{ill|1941|en|1941|WD=Q5231|lt=1941}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 16), under the {{ill|Metal Collection Order|ja|金属類回収令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Metal Collection Order|1=Metal Collection Order|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646956}}, two statues of the {{ill|Divine Horse Statue|ja|神馬|lt=Divine Horse Statue|lt_ja=神馬像|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Horse Statue|1=shinme|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11591047}} were requisitioned for arms production, but the memorial tower remained. == Access == * Get off at {{ill|Hanshin Electric Railway|en|Hanshin Electric Railway|WD=Q1140781|lt=Hanshin Electric Railway}}’s {{ill|Naruo - Mukogawajoshidai-Mae Station|en|Naruo - Mukogawajoshidai-Mae Station|WD=Q6966460|lt=Naruo/Mukogawa Women's University-mae Station}} and walk about 3 minutes to the northwest. * Get off at Hanshin’s {{ill|Kōshien Station|en|Kōshien Station|WD=Q6455288|lt=Koshien Station}} and walk about 10 minutes east. {{clear|right}} == Footnotes == === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注"}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book |language=Japanese |last= |first= |author= |authorlink= |coauthors= |translator= |editor= Naruo Village Chronicles Editorial Committee|date=2005-03 |title=Naruo Village Chronicles 1885-1951 |publisher=Nishinomiya City Naruo Ward Asset Management Committee |page= |id= |isbn= |quote= |ref=鳴尾村誌}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okata Shrine|ja|岡太神社 (西宮市)|lt=Okata Shrine|lt_ja=岡太神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okata Shrine|1=Okata Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11472009}} == External Links == * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6302025.html Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – Hyōgo Prefecture {{ill|Shrine Agency|ja|神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Agency|1=Jinja-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590703}} * [https://yaokami.jp/1280901/ Naruo Hachiman Shrine] – [https://yaokami.jp/ Eight Million Gods] * Naruo Hachiman Shrine Volunteer Association {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|鳴尾八幡神社|version=103027411|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q22119811}} [[Category:Buildings in Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Nishinomiya, Hyogo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] [[ja:鳴尾八幡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22119811]] Wikidata name: Naruo hachiman-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |名称 = Naruo Hachiman Shrine (鳴尾八幡神社) |画像 = [[File:2015_1103_Naruo_Hachiman_Shaden.jpg|280px|2015th year 11月3日撮影]]
社殿 |所在地 = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}}上鳴尾町14-21 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|43|20|N|135|22|8.3|E|region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇太神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Omikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} |神体 = |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝・{{ill|Bun'an|en|Bun'an|WD=Q1193586|lt=Bun'an era}} |本殿 = |別名 = {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月9日 |神事 = 例大祭 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=20|lon_deg=135|lon_min=22|lon_sec=8.3|label=鳴尾八幡神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |image = File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Shaden.jpg |prefecture = {{ill|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.722222|N|135.368972|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} }} '''Naruo Hachiman Shrine''' (鳴尾八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=Nishinomiya City}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is venerated by local residents as the {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|WD=Q97479976|lt=Guardian Deity}} of the Naruo district of the city. Regarding its {{ill|Shrine Name|ja|社号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Name|1=Shrine Name|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588355}}, it is sometimes simply referred to as '''Hachiman Shrine'''{{ill|Naruo Village Chronicles|ja|鳴尾村誌|lt=Naruo Village Chronicles 1889-1951|lt_ja=鳴尾村誌 1889-1951|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naruo Village Chronicles|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} P.103-104, and the nameplate at the entrance is inscribed with “八幡神社.” == History == [[File:2015 1103 Naruo Hachiman Hengaku.jpg|thumb|200px|有栖川宮熾仁親王より奉献された勅額（複製）]] The founding is said to have occurred during the {{ill|Bun'an|en|Bun'an|WD=Q1193586|lt=Bun'an era}} when {{ill|Emperor Go-Hanazono|en|Emperor Go-Hanazono|WD=Q451926|lt=Emperor Gokaen}} reigned. Its old name was '''Hirohata Hachiman Shrine''' (廣幡八幡宮).
